feedback: ['I encountered an issue when hitting the "Shift" key when I began to type.\xa0 If my finger was too close to the "Enter" key, it was registering as my having hit the "Continue" button, which was not my intention.\xa0 I believe this happened on four occasions, so my "reason" was missing on these four entries.\xa0 I did find it confusing that it was not made more clear that participants were supposed to be focusing on physical characteristics when giving reasons.\xa0 I was coming up with things based on certain colors, but also why someone\'s fertility may have been higher or lower in general, not having anything to do with appearance.\xa0 Maybe I\'m the only one who found it confusing (it\'s been a long day).\xa0 Good luck with your research!']

["She wasn't trying hard enough, and just needs to get out there more.", 'Perhaps a richer diet and more bioavailable nutrients led to an increase of fertility.', 'Mated during a drought when resources were scarce; there was a small attack on her nest where she lost 2 eggs.', "Lola (we're calling her Lola) did her best, but her mate had been malnourished and produced sperm of poor quality.", "Without help from her mate, Selma (we're calling her Selma) was able to construct a moderate nesting site, but still hit some hiccups along the way.", 'Gertrude excelled at finding a proper nesting site, and environmental factors were on her side. Good job, Gertrude.', 'This young and virile creature, Daisy, came into breeding age last year, and still had plenty to give this year.', "I've lost count of frogs and names. This might be Alice, and Alice might have done her best with a mediocre mate, and the fertility score sort of met in the middle.", "Alice is beginning to show us that this is her patterning, and 5 is her reaching for the stars. That's okay, Alice. Ordinary isn't a bad thing.", 'Lola, as a show girl, was overworked and underpaid. (Resources were scarce this year; she only produced 3 good eggs)', 'I think this one was Daisy. Daisy is dark green and an egg-cellent egg layer. She eats her greens (abundant food) and her nest is always well made and away from danger.', "Lola is just egg-xhausted at this point in her career. She's aging, and it's beginning to show.", 'Daisy continues to be well-nourished and have access to easy, protected nesting sites. With little competition, she faces less challenges.', "Was this Daisy? Daisy's sister, Gertrude? The green lizards are doing well. The green keeps them camoflaugged better than the bright red lizards, so their nesting sites are less prone to intruders.", 'Maybe these lizards are like bell peppers, and the redder they are the older they are. Or, the red makes them easy for predators to spot them.', "Red is a recessive gene that affects fertility. Maybe this was Gertrude. I can't remember.", 'Lola is the reddest of them all, and constant scoring low. Red affects fertility.', 'Light green probably signals a younger, more fertile lizard. Dark green is matured?', "She's starting to fade OR the males don't like pink.", 'What if this is the entire life cycle of one lizard, from young and green and fertile to old and withering bright red Lola.', 'Lola pulled through a little bit. I stand corrected. Bright red can be attractive and possible not a sign of aging?', 'Wow, pink is back in. I stand corrected. Are these lizards going through beauty trends?', 'I should have named her fertile Myrtle. Juno reference. Consistently fertile, with no apparent changes to mating habits or environment.', "Gasp! Daisy! Maybe she's tired. She's had a lot of kids. Perhaps there was a drought again."]
